title: Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena: Exposing the Truth
location: 2154 Rayburn House Office Building, Washington, D.C.
subcommittee: Joint hearing of the Subcommittee on Cybersecurity, Information Technology, and Government Innovation and the Subcommittee on National Security, the Border, and Foreign Affairs
summary: A joint House Oversight hearing examining government transparency, alleged secret UAP programs, aviation and maritime safety, scientific study, whistleblower concerns, and congressional access to UAP information.
whyItMatters: This hearing extended the House’s public UAP oversight beyond the July 2023 witness panel. It added former defense, Navy, NASA, and journalism perspectives and created another documented bridge among transparency, aviation safety, scientific investigation, alleged hidden programs, and proposed disclosure legislation.
editorialNote: GreyAlien distinguishes the official hearing record from witness assertions. Statements below summarize testimony and do not independently establish every claim made during the hearing.

witnesses.name: Luis Elizondo
witnesses.roleAtHearing: Author and former Department of Defense official
witnesses.testimonySummary: Elizondo argued that excessive secrecy and limited oversight had obstructed public understanding of UAP-related activities and called for stronger congressional oversight, whistleblower protections, and a coordinated national strategy.
keyPoints: He advocated for a whole-of-government approach to UAP policy.
He called for stronger protections for people reporting UAP-related information.
He alleged that some UAP-related activities had operated outside adequate public and congressional oversight.

witnesses.name: Rear Admiral Tim Gallaudet, Ph.D. (Ret.)
witnesses.roleAtHearing: Chief Executive Officer, Ocean STL Consulting, LLC
witnesses.background: Retired U.S. Navy rear admiral and former NOAA official
witnesses.testimonySummary: Gallaudet described a 2015 Navy safety-of-flight episode involving the GoFast video, argued that overclassification had hindered aviation and maritime safety, and called for greater transparency and stronger congressional oversight.
keyPoints: He described receiving a Navy message concerning an urgent safety-of-flight issue.
He connected UAP reporting to aviation and maritime safety.
He supported renewed disclosure legislation and broader scientific study.

witnesses.name: Michael Gold
witnesses.roleAtHearing: Former NASA Associate Administrator of Space Policy and Partnerships
witnesses.background: Member of NASA’s UAP Independent Study Team
witnesses.testimonySummary: Gold focused on the value of scientific inquiry, improved data collection, reduced stigma, and cooperation among government, academia, and the private sector.
keyPoints: He emphasized rigorous scientific study and better-quality data.
He discussed NASA’s role in reducing stigma around UAP research.
He encouraged collaboration across public and private institutions.

witnesses.name: Michael Shellenberger
witnesses.testimonySummary: Shellenberger discussed allegations that the Department of Defense and Intelligence Community were withholding UAP-related information from Congress and submitted supporting material for the hearing record.
keyPoints: He argued that congressional oversight had been impeded by secrecy.
He discussed claims concerning alleged UAP programs.
He submitted extensive supporting material into the official hearing record.

timeline.label: Hearing first published in the House Committee Repository
Public hearing held at 11:30 a.m. in 2154 Rayburn
Official transcript added to the House Committee Repository

evidenceProfile.summary: The hearing, transcript, witness statements, and support documents are official records. Individual claims and interpretations require separate evaluation.
